signin.title: Please enter your device admin password...

save.title: Saving configuration
save.waitmsg: Please wait until process is finished...

Status.memWarn: Too less memory to update via OTA ! $(flash_used_percent)% used.

Restart.title: Restart Device
Restart.desc: Restart the device. Save your changes, otherwise they will be lost.
Restart.confirm: Restart the device now? - all unsaved changes will be lost!
Restart.waitmsg: Please wait while the device is restarting...

Reset.title: Factory Reset
Reset.desc: All settings will be dropped, the device will reboot with factory settings.
Reset.confirm: Are you sure you want to reset the device to factory defaults? The device will restart in delivery state.
Reset.waitmsg: Please wait while the device is resetting to factory defaults...

mode.info: You can run your device in AP Mode or Client Mode. In client mode you will need to connect to an existing Wi-Fi network, in AP Mode ESP creates a Wi-Fi network by itself.
mode.label: Mode

bssid.info: Select the BSSID to use explicit node (optional).
bssid.label: BSSID

hide.info: Hide the SSID of the access point.
hide.label: Hide SSID

dhcp.info: Use DHCP or static ip addresses.
dhcp.label: Use DHCP

ip.info: Set the static IP address of this node.
ip.label: IP Address

subnet.info: Set the subnet mask to be used.
subnet.label: Subnet mask

gw.info: Address of the Gateway to the world.
gw.label: Gateway

fallback.info: Fallback to Access Point Mode when WiFi Network could not be joined.
fallback.label: Fallback

STA.desc: Type your Wi-Fi Network's SSID or press 'Scan' for nearby Wireless Networks to join.

desc.enable: Enable the message broker to use this service
desc.configure: Configure the message broker or disable to stop the service

enable.label: MQTT Support
enable.info: Enable or disable the MQTT support

host.label: Host
host.info: MQTT Server host name, IP address or url

passwd.label: Password
passwd.info: Password of the technical login user

topic.label: Topic prefix
topic.info: Prefix applied to all MQTT topics

autotopic.label: Auto topic
autotopic.info: Automatically add a unique identifier (last 6 digits of MAC address) to the MQTT topic prefix

syncrate.label: Sync time (seconds)
syncrate.info: Sync time to send a heartbeat message to the MQTT server

Device.title: Set the name of your device (hostname) and the password for the admin account

password.title: Enter the password for the admin account

SysCfg.title:
Backup or Restore the device configuration. Be aware, the backup file is NOT encrypted.
Store it on a safe place to avoid loosing your access credentials...

Backup.title: Backup Settings
Backup.desc: Save the config to a backup file.

Restore.title: Restore Settings
Restore.desc: Click and choose your file from your local device to be restored.
Restore.invalidFile: The file you selected is not a valid backup file...

Firmware.title: Firmware update is possible. Make a backup first, before updating to avoid loss of data.
Firmware.install: Select the version you want to install
Firmware.noInternet: It seems, there is no internet connection available or the firmware update server is down.

Feedback.desc: You have trouble with the software? You have a feature request? Don't worry, contact the development team...
Feedback.docText: Open the online documentation (maybe your question is already answered there).
Feedback.emailText: Send a mail to contact us (it's not possible to send gifts with mail, but you can ask how to transfer 🙈).
Feedback.githubText: Open an issue on GitHub (for bugs and feature requests).

enable.info: Enable or disable the RF433 function

OnAir.desc: Setup the behaviour of your OnAir Lamp.

mic.info: Select the action, when an active microphone is detected

cam.info: Select the action, when an active camera is detected

priority.info: Does the the camera or the microphone setting has priority, if both are on

bright.info: Select the maximal brightness in percent

Clients.desc:
Download and install the client for your operating system.
As most of the clients are signed, you can use and install the certificates first.

Certs.desc:
To limit the risk, the software is signed with certificates from LSC-Labs.
Install the root and the intermediate certificates from first - be sure you are installing it from the correct page.

html:
The available client software can be found on the download page.
The software is available for Windows, Linux and MacOS - (under construction).
For Windows, there are several different versions available: One click deploy (MS) and portable versions for .Net and Net Framework.
<li>The one click deploy version is the easiest way to install the software, but it is not the most secure way to do it.
To minimize this risk, this versions are signed, so you can use them without worry about.
• The portable versions are a good option if you want to use the software on a USB stick or if you don't want to change the target system.
